question_text: Verify the address of the emergency. What is the exact address?
What is the callback number you are calling from?
Tell me exactly what happened.
Is s/he seizing right now?
Is s/he conscious?
Is s/he breathing?
How long has the seizure been going on?
What does the seizure look like — is the whole body shaking or just one part?
Has the seizure stopped? Is s/he waking up?
Is s/he responsive — can s/he answer questions or follow commands?
Does s/he have a history of seizures or epilepsy?
Does s/he take seizure medication? Has s/he missed any doses?
Has s/he had more than one seizure today?
Did s/he hit the head during the seizure?
Is there any bleeding or injury?
Is s/he diabetic? Could the blood sugar be low?
Does s/he use drugs or alcohol? Could this be an overdose or withdrawal?
Does s/he have a fever? Is s/he hot to the touch?
Is the patient pregnant?
How old is the patient?
Has s/he been ill recently?
ECHO-level: Seizure with respiratory arrest — not breathing
DELTA-level: Status epilepticus — continuous seizure > 5 minutes
DELTA-level: Multiple seizures without regaining consciousness between them
DELTA-level: Seizure in pregnant patient (eclampsia)
DELTA-level: First-time seizure with no known seizure history
condition: no seizure history AND first event

question_text: CHARLIE-level: Seizure stopped, postictal, known epilepsy, breathing normally
condition: postictal AND known epilepsy AND breathing

question_text: CHARLIE-level: Seizure with head injury during event
BRAVO-level: Known epilepsy, single seizure, recovering normally, missed medication
condition: known epilepsy AND single seizure AND recovering AND missed meds

question_text: BRAVO-level: Febrile seizure in child, seizure has stopped, breathing normally
condition: febrile AND child AND seizure stopped AND breathing

question_text: ALPHA-level: Known epilepsy, typical seizure pattern, fully recovered, back to baseline
condition: known epilepsy AND fully recovered AND at baseline

question_text: OMEGA-level: Suspected non-epileptic event, fully alert, no injury, physician follow-up
condition: non-epileptic AND alert AND no injury

question_text: Do NOT put anything in the patient's mouth. Do NOT restrain them.
Clear the area around the patient — move furniture and sharp objects away.
Time the seizure if possible. Note when it started.
When the seizure stops, gently roll the patient onto their side (recovery position).
If the patient is diabetic, do NOT give anything by mouth while altered. Wait until fully alert.
If the patient stops breathing after the seizure, begin CPR.
Stay on the line. Help is on the way.
If another seizure starts or s/he stops breathing, call us back immediately.
